---
title: "Installation"
---

## Python development environment

This repository uses **conda and Poetry together**. The conda environment is
named `admixture`.

```bash
conda env create -f conda.yaml
conda activate admixture
poetry config virtualenvs.create false
poetry install --with dev
```

If the environment already exists:

```bash
conda activate admixture
conda env update -f conda.yaml --prune
poetry install --with dev
```

## Quarto documentation engine

The documentation in `docs/` is a Quarto website. The conda environment includes
the Quarto CLI when created from `conda.yaml`.

Render locally with:

```bash
conda activate admixture
quarto render docs
```

Preview locally with:

```bash
quarto preview docs
```

## Julia runtime

Julia is an external runtime, not a Python dependency.

Install Julia from <https://julialang.org/downloads/> and verify it is on
`PATH`:

```bash
julia --version
```

You can also pass an explicit executable path:

```python
from admixture import OpenAdmixtureRunner

runner = OpenAdmixtureRunner(julia="/path/to/julia")
```

On Windows:

```python
runner = OpenAdmixtureRunner(
    julia=r"C:\Users\you\AppData\Local\Programs\Julia-1.11.0\bin\julia.exe"
)
```

## OpenADMIXTURE.jl

Install the upstream Julia packages into your selected Julia environment:

```bash
julia -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.add(url="https://github.com/kose-y/SparseKmeansFeatureRanking.jl"); Pkg.add(url="https://github.com/OpenMendel/OpenADMIXTURE.jl")'
```

For a project-local Julia environment:

```bash
mkdir julia_env
julia --project=julia_env -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.add(url="https://github.com/kose-y/SparseKmeansFeatureRanking.jl"); Pkg.add(url="https://github.com/OpenMendel/OpenADMIXTURE.jl")'
```

Alternatively, bootstrap the project-local Julia environment explicitly from
Python:

```python
import admixture

admixture.setup(project_dir="julia_env")
```

Then configure the runner:

```python
runner = OpenAdmixtureRunner(project_dir="julia_env")
```
